name: Make sure secrets are not passed as container environment variables
body:
### Overview

This control ensures that **Amazon ECS task definitions do not pass sensitive AWS credentials as plain environment variables**.

### What is checked

The assessment reviews active ECS task definitions and identifies container environment entries containing sensitive keys such as AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, or ECS_ENGINE_AUTH_DATA.

### Why it matters

Plain environment variables can be exposed through task definitions, logs, crash dumps, local process inspection, debugging tools, and overly broad read permissions. Static AWS credentials in containers also bypass the safer task-role model.

### What good looks like

Secrets should come from AWS Secrets Manager, Systems Manager Parameter Store SecureString values, or the ECS secrets block. AWS API access should use ECS task roles instead of static access keys.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

### AWS

Move secrets out of plain ECS container environment variables. This control identifies active ECS task definitions where containerDefinitions[].environment contains sensitive keys such as AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, or ECS_ENGINE_AUTH_DATA.

1. Open the affected ECS task definition revision.
2. Remove sensitive values from containerDefinitions[].environment.
3. Store the secret in AWS Secrets Manager or Systems Manager Parameter Store as a SecureString.
4. Reference the secret from containerDefinitions[].secrets.
5. Replace static AWS credentials with an ECS task role whenever possible.
6. Register a new task definition revision and update the ECS service to use it.
7. Force a new deployment so running tasks start with the corrected definition.

Task definition pattern:

~~~json
{
  "containerDefinitions": [
    {
      "name": "app",
      "secrets": [
        {
          "name": "APP_SECRET",
          "valueFrom": "arn:aws:secretsmanager:<region>:<account-id>:secret:<secret-name>"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
~~~

Update the service after registering the new revision:

~~~bash
aws ecs update-service \
  --cluster <cluster-name> \
  --service <service-name> \
  --task-definition <task-definition-family>:<revision> \
  --force-new-deployment \
  --region {{asset.region}}
~~~

References:
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/specifying-sensitive-data.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task-iam-roles.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/APIReference/API_Secret.html

The control is satisfied when the active task definition no longer contains flagged secret keys in plain environment variables.
description: Amazon ECS task definitions that pass sensitive values as plain container environment variables.
